activity:
09:00 – Experimental Run: BEC Formation and Interferometry  
He initiates the condensate sequence: laser cooling, evaporative cooling in a hybrid trap, and final magnetic confinement. Over 12 minutes, the cloud of 500,000 atoms cools to 80 nK, forming a coherent BEC. He then launches the condensate into a double-well optical lattice and applies a π/2 pulse to create a matter-wave interferometer. Using a high-resolution CCD camera, he captures the interference fringes—each fringe representing a quantum phase difference across the condensate. He records the visibility of the fringes, which he hopes will reveal subtle decoherence effects induced by residual atomic interactions.
prompt: Why does the condensate seem to “breathe” slightly after the π/2 pulse, as if the atoms are whispering to each other through hidden entanglement channels I can’t yet detect?
